title: Context & tooling
intro: Infrastructure and tooling used to store, retrieve, compress, and serve context for LLM applications.

items.name: Ollama
items.description: Open-source tool designed to run, manage, and serve open-weight language models locally.
features: Simple setup for running open-weight models such as Llama and Mistral.
Built-in library of optimized model weights.
Supports local API access for integration with other tools.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: vLLM
items.description: High-throughput and memory-efficient serving engine for LLMs.
features: PagedAttention algorithm for efficient KV cache management.
Supports continuous batching and high-concurrency requests.
Compatible with many open-weight model architectures.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: llama.cpp
items.description: LLM inference implementation in pure C/C++ for high-performance execution on a variety of hardware.
features: Minimal dependencies and high portability across CPUs and GPUs.
Supports advanced quantization techniques to reduce memory footprint.
Foundational component for many other local LLM applications.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: LM Studio
items.description: Desktop application that allows users to discover, download, and run local LLMs.
features: Graphical interface for model discovery and management.
Local inference server with OpenAI-compatible API.
Hardware-accelerated execution on multiple platforms.
items.pricing: Free for personal use.

items.name: Text Generation Inference (TGI)
items.description: A toolkit for deploying and serving LLMs developed by Hugging Face.
features: Optimized for production deployments with support for many model architectures.
Features including tensor parallelism and token streaming.
Integrated with the Hugging Face ecosystem for model discovery.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use under the HFOIL license.

items.name: Vellum
items.description: Integrated platform combining prompt design, evaluation, and observability for LLM-powered applications.
features: Visual prompt editor with experiment tracking and A/B testing.
Centralized management of model variants and datasets.
Production deployment tools with performance monitoring.
items.pricing: Free tier for small projects; paid plans based on usage and team seats.

items.name: PromptPerfect
items.description: Prompt optimization tool that refines and compresses prompts for efficiency and accuracy across models.
features: Automatic prompt tuning for different LLMs.
Reduces token count while maintaining quality.
Integrates via API for programmatic optimization.
items.pricing: Free tier available; subscription plans for professional and enterprise users.

items.name: OpenAI Playground
items.description: Interactive web interface to experiment with OpenAI models and test prompts.
features: Real-time prompt testing with adjustable parameters.
Supports GPT-5.5 and fine-tuned models.
Integrated API preview and token usage display.
items.pricing: Free access with an OpenAI account; pay-as-you-go billing for API requests.

items.name: Google AI Studio
items.description: Web-based environment for experimenting with Gemini models, testing prompts, and moving from prompt prototypes to Gemini API code.
features: Interactive prompt testing for Gemini models.
Supports multimodal experimentation.
Provides code export for Gemini API integration.
items.pricing: Free access with a Google account; API usage is billed separately when prototypes are moved to production.

items.name: Anthropic Console
items.description: Web-based interface for experimenting with Claude models and optimizing prompts.
features: Prompt experimentation with the Claude 4 and Claude 5 models.
Adjustable system prompts and context size.
Conversation history and export for reproducible tests.
items.pricing: Free access with a Claude account; API billing applies to extended or automated testing.

items.name: Helicone
items.description: Observability and prompt analytics platform for LLM-based applications.
features: Centralized logging of prompts and responses.
Prompt version tracking tied to runtime requests.
Cost, latency, and quality analytics across model variants.
items.pricing: Free tier for individual developers; paid plans based on request volume and team features.

items.name: Repo Prompt
items.description: Context engineering tool and MCP-enabled platform for assembling token-efficient prompts from code repositories.
features: Selects and structures relevant files into optimized prompts for LLMs.
Supports planning workflows and reviewable diffs for code changes.
Provides token estimation and context slicing for large codebases.
items.pricing: Free tier available; paid plans for advanced features.

items.name: PromptLayer
items.description: Dedicated prompt management platform designed to version and analyze prompts used in LLM-powered applications.
features: Prompt versioning with diffs and historical comparisons.
Association of prompts with models and datasets.
Runtime tracking and analytics via lightweight SDKs.
items.pricing: Free tier with basic functionality; usage-based pricing for production workloads and teams.

items.name: Mirascope
items.description: Open-source Python framework that combines prompt management and evaluation.
features: Prompt definitions expressed as composable Python constructs.
Tight integration between prompts and function calling.
Built-in support for testing prompt behavior.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; commercial support and enterprise offerings available.

items.name: Lilypad
items.description: Prompt operations and observability platform focused on tracing and evaluating prompts.
features: Prompt tracing with full request and response context.
Versioned prompt experiments with evaluation hooks.
Integration with common LLM providers and frameworks.
items.pricing: Free tier for experimentation; paid plans based on usage.

items.name: Agenta
items.description: Open-source LLMOps platform for building and monitoring LLM-powered applications.
features: Unified platform for prompt engineering and versioning.
Built-in evaluation workflows including LLM-as-a-judge capabilities.
Observability and tracing of inputs and system performance.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use for self-hosting; managed cloud offering available.

items.name: Pinecone
items.description: Fully managed vector database service optimized for similarity search.
features: Serverless and fully managed architecture.
Real-time vector search with metadata filtering.
High scalability and strong ecosystem integrations.
items.pricing: Usage-based pricing; free tier available for smaller projects.

items.name: Milvus
items.description: Open-source vector database built for massive embedding and similarity search workloads.
features: Distributed architecture for large-scale deployments.
GPU acceleration for faster search.
Integration with AI frameworks and cloud hosting options.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; paid managed option via Zilliz Cloud.

items.name: Weaviate
items.description: Open-source vector database supporting semantic and hybrid search.
features: Hybrid vector and keyword search support.
Built-in REST and GraphQL APIs for context queries.
Managed and self-hosted deployment options.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use for self-hosting; managed cloud service with usage-based pricing.

items.name: Qdrant
items.description: Open-source vector search engine focused on reliability and high performance.
features: High-performance similarity search engine.
REST and gRPC APIs for easy integration.
Ready for on-premise and managed cloud environments.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; managed Qdrant Cloud with tiered pricing.

items.name: Chroma
items.description: Lightweight vector store designed for prototyping and local development.
features: Simple setup with local or embedded mode.
Tight integration with popular LLM frameworks.
Supports persistence and metadata filtering.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: FAISS
items.description: High-performance library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vector embeddings.
features: Optimized nearest-neighbor search for large-scale collections.
Multiple indexing strategies balancing recall and latency.
CPU and GPU implementations for high-throughput retrieval.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: Zvec
items.description: Open-source, in-process vector database designed to embed directly into applications.
features: Runs entirely in-process with no server or network dependency.
Supports dense and sparse embeddings and hybrid search.
Built on Alibaba Proxima engine for high-performance retrieval.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: text-embedding-3-large
items.description: OpenAI's highest-quality embedding model designed for semantic search and RAG tasks.
features: State-of-the-art quality across retrieval benchmarks.
High consistency across domains such as code and documents.
Excellent performance for English-language search.
items.lens: API-hosted model optimized for high-quality semantic retrieval.
items.pricing: API-based pricing per 1 million tokens; pay-as-you-go usage.

items.name: Cohere Embed v3
items.description: A multilingual embedding model optimized for enterprise search and RAG pipelines.
features: Very strong multilingual performance.
Long-context input support.
Optimized for high-recall search.
items.lens: API-hosted multilingual model aimed at enterprise search.
items.pricing: API usage pricing per 1 million tokens; enterprise plans available.

items.name: BGE-large
items.description: Open-source embedding models known for high retrieval accuracy.
features: Excellent performance among open-source models.
Suitable for local and privacy-sensitive workloads.
Strong semantic retrieval with a small footprint.
items.lens: Self-hostable open model family commonly used for English retrieval.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: jina-embeddings-v3
items.description: Open-source embedding model designed for long-context documents and multilingual tasks.
features: Long-context support up to approximately 64k tokens.
Multilingual embeddings with consistent quality.
Optimized for generative search and RAG.
items.lens: Self-hostable or API-hosted multilingual model with long-input support.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; optional hosted API available.

items.name: Neo4j
items.description: The industry-standard native graph database used for its maturity and performance.
features: Native graph storage processing with the Cypher query language.
Integrated vector search for hybrid retrieval.
Rich ecosystem including Graph Data Science library.
items.pricing: Free Community Edition; managed cloud service with free and paid tiers.

items.name: Amazon Neptune
items.description: A fully managed graph database service built for the AWS cloud, designed for high availability.
features: Support for Property Graph and RDF data models.
Neptune Analytics for analyzing graph data in memory.
Deep integration with the AWS ecosystem.
items.pricing: Pay-per-hour for instances or serverless usage.

items.name: ArangoDB
items.description: A native multi-model database that combines graph, document, and key-value data models.
features: Unified Query Language for graphs and vector search.
Scalable architecture with SmartGraphs for distributed processing.
Multi-model support for combining metadata filtering with graph traversals.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; managed cloud service available.

items.name: OpenRAG
items.description: End-to-end open-source RAG platform that integrates document ingestion and conversational interfaces.
features: Pre-integrated RAG stack including ingestion and chat interface.
Visual workflow builder for rapid iteration.
Agentic RAG workflows with re-ranking.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: RAGFlow
items.description: Open-source RAG engine that combines document understanding and agent orchestration.
features: Integrated ingestion and retrieval pipelines.
Advanced document parsing and hybrid search capabilities.
Support for agent workflows and multi-source data ingestion.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: PageIndex
items.description: Framework for reasoning-based retrieval that organizes documents into hierarchical structures.
features: Eliminates vector databases and chunking through structured indexing.
Builds hierarchical table-of-contents representations for long documents.
Enables reasoning-based retrieval with improved traceability.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: Airweave
items.description: Context retrieval layer that connects external data sources and exposes them through a unified search interface.
features: Continuous data synchronization from multiple tools and databases.
Unified retrieval API for agents and AI systems.
Handles ingestion and access control across heterogeneous sources.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: Mem0
items.description: Advanced memory layer for LLM agents that enables persistent and structured context across sessions.
features: Multi-level memory including user and session states.
Smart memory extraction and consolidation.
Token-efficient retrieval for long-term context.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; enterprise features available.

items.name: Zep
items.description: Context engineering platform that builds temporal knowledge graphs to manage user and conversation data.
features: Graph-based memory representation of entities and relationships.
Dynamic context assembly optimized for token efficiency.
Scalable enterprise-grade memory persistence.
items.pricing: Free developer tier; paid enterprise plans available.

items.name: Cognee
items.description: Hybrid memory engine combining graph and vector stores for reasoning-driven context management.
features: Unified graph plus vector memory architecture.
Custom pipelines for reasoning and domain adaptation.
Designed for multi-agent and enterprise workflows.
items.pricing: Free trial available; subscription plans for advanced features.

items.name: Letta
items.description: Persistent memory framework enabling LLM agents to manage and update internal state over long conversations.
features: Self-reflective memory for adaptive agent behavior.
Supports local and remote storage backends.
Compatible with popular LLM frameworks.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; paid hosting available.

items.name: LangMem
items.description: Modular memory management component that provides flexible context persistence for LLM agents.
features: Supports multiple storage backends, including Redis and Postgres.
Integrates with LangChain agents and tools.
Customizable strategies for context summarization.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use; enterprise hosting via LangSmith.

items.name: MemPalace
items.description: Memory management framework designed to structure and retrieve contextual information for AI systems.
features: Organizes context into structured and hierarchical memory spaces.
Enables selective retrieval of information based on semantic associations.
Designed to reduce context fragmentation in AI workflows.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: OpenViking
items.description: Open-source long-context management framework focused on compressing and retrieving large-scale context.
features: Context compression and summarization for long documents.
Efficient retrieval mechanisms for large-scale contextual data.
Designed to support long-context reasoning.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.

items.name: Memori
items.description: Open-source memory engine using SQL-native storage to provide transparent agent memory.
features: Local-first memory management using SQLite or PostgreSQL.
Automatic entity and relationship extraction.
Simple integration for lightweight agent deployments.
items.pricing: Open-source and free to use.
